Major
Microsoft

Microsoft Unveils Full MAI Model Family at Build 2026 — Seven Proprietary Models Reduce OpenAI Dependency

Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman introduced seven MAI models at Build 2026 to reduce reliance on OpenAI APIs. The family includes MAI-Thinking-1 (sparse ~1T-parameter MoE reasoning model, trained entirely on commercially licensed data), MAI-Code-1-Flash (5B, integrating into VS Code and GitHub Copilot), MAI-Image-2.5, MAI-Transcribe-1.5, and MAI-Voice-2 (15 languages). All models are available through Azure AI Foundry and integrated across Microsoft 365 Copilot Wave 4.

Why it mattersSeven in-house models built without distilling OpenAI marks Microsoft's structural move toward AI self-sufficiency — making it simultaneously a major OpenAI customer and direct competitor.

Notable
WeRide

WeRide, Uber, and AVOMO Launch Spain's First Commercial Robotaxi Pilot in Madrid

WeRide, Uber, and Madrid fleet partner AVOMO announced Spain's first commercial robotaxi pilot, with rides available through the Uber app in Madrid's urban core. The service will initially include safety operators before scaling to fully driverless operation. Madrid is the fourth of 15 cities targeted under the WeRide–Uber global agreement, which aims to expand across Europe, the Middle East, and Asia by 2030.

Why it mattersThe first Spanish commercial robotaxi pilot establishes a EU regulatory precedent and validates WeRide's global partnership model for reaching autonomous-vehicle scale outside China.

2 Jun 2026

Notable
NeurIPS

NeurIPS 2026 Desk-Rejects 178 Papers via AI Detector — False Positive Backlash Erupts

The NeurIPS 2026 Position Paper Track ran 969 submissions through Pangram v3.3.2, desk-rejecting 178 papers and requiring 123 more to prove human authorship by June 15. Researchers — including track chairs whose own papers scored 24–69% AI-probability — reported that heavy human editing still triggered high false-positive scores. The controversy reignited debate about whether AI-detection tools meet the reliability threshold for consequential academic gatekeeping.

Why it mattersAI detectors are shaping access to publication at top ML venues — their known calibration issues make this a systemic risk for legitimate researchers and a policy failure at one of the field's most influential conferences.

NeurIPSAI PolicyResearchPeer ReviewAI DetectionAcademic

Major
Bpifrance

Bpifrance, Mistral, and MGX Expand Campus AI to 3 GW Nationwide — Second French Site Selected

On June 2, the Campus AI joint venture — comprising Bpifrance, Mistral AI, MGX (Abu Dhabi), and NVIDIA — announced the imminent selection of a second French site, doubling its initial investment and targeting 3 GW of AI compute capacity across a pan-European network of AI factories. The first flagship campus at Fouju in Seine-et-Marne is already underway; construction at the second site is scheduled to begin H2 2026, with operations targeted for 2028. The expansion reinforces France's position as Europe's primary destination for large-scale, decarbonised AI compute.

Why it mattersCampus AI's 3 GW national buildout — backed by public and Gulf sovereign capital alongside NVIDIA hardware — is the most concrete execution vehicle for France's ambition to become Europe's sovereign AI compute hub by 2028.

2 Jun 2026

Notable
Polish Government

Poland PM Tusk Announces 'Sovereignty Test' for Government Tech Purchases at European Financial Congress

At the European Financial Congress in Sopot on June 2, Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk announced that Poland will introduce a mandatory 'sovereignty test' for significant state purchases of technology, requiring annual public reports on IT independence progress. Tusk warned that Poland's dependency on a narrow group of foreign technology suppliers had reached dangerous proportions and cited France, Denmark, and Germany as models to emulate. The government simultaneously confirmed a nearly €500 million allocation — mostly from EU funds — to deploy AI laboratories in 12,000 Polish schools.

Why it mattersPoland's sovereignty test introduces procurement-level AI nationalism to Central Europe, likely triggering similar measures in the Czech Republic, Slovakia, and the Baltic states as the region seeks to reduce US tech concentration risk.

2 Jun 2026

Major
European Commission

EU €20B AI Gigafactory Programme Stumbles — Bids Drop from 70 to ~10, Funding Round Pushed to July

Bloomberg reported on June 2 that the EU's €20 billion InvestAI gigafactory programme — designed to build at least five 100,000-chip, 1 GW AI data centres — is facing a severe credibility crisis. The bidding round originally scheduled for May was deferred to July, and only two of the five centres can receive subsidy disbursements before the EU's 2028 budget cycle; interest from potential bidders has shrunk from 70 companies to roughly 10, with at least two consortia reconsidering entirely. Schwarz Group and Deutsche Telekom have dampened enthusiasm and are proceeding with independent data centre investments instead.

Why it mattersThe gigafactory programme's stumble reveals that public subsidy timelines (2028/2030) are misaligned with private-sector AI infrastructure investment cycles, threatening to cede the European compute buildout entirely to Gulf and US sovereign capital.

EUAI InfrastructureGigafactoryPolicyData CentersInvestAIFunding

2 Jun 2026

Major
OpenAI

OpenAI Revokes All macOS Code-Signing Certificates Following TanStack Supply Chain Fallout

OpenAI announced it is revoking all macOS code-signing certificates on June 12, 2026, requiring all users of ChatGPT Desktop, Codex App, Codex CLI, and Atlas to update before the deadline or their apps will stop working. The action follows the May 11 Mini Shai-Hulud worm that compromised two OpenAI employee devices via poisoned @tanstack/* npm packages. OpenAI found no evidence of user data exfiltration, making this the second certificate rotation in two months — an earlier Axios incident triggered the first rotation in April.

Why it mattersBack-to-back supply chain incidents reveal that AI model release pipelines remain outside the scope of standard red-team evaluations, leaving build and release infrastructure systemically unaudited.

Major
Cognition

Cognition Launches Devin Desktop — Agent-Neutral IDE Built on Windsurf Rebrand with ACP Support

Cognition rebranded Windsurf as Devin Desktop, a full code editor and agent orchestration layer for engineering teams. The update introduces Devin Local (rewritten in Rust with 30% greater token efficiency), native support for the open Agent Client Protocol (ACP), and out-of-the-box integrations with OpenAI Codex, Claude Agent, and OpenCode. The move positions Cognition — which raised $1B at a $25B valuation in May 2026 — to compete directly with GitHub Copilot and Cursor at the IDE layer.

Why it mattersStandardizing the Agent Client Protocol across multiple coding agents could define how enterprise software development is orchestrated in the agentic era, challenging GitHub Copilot's platform dominance.

Oracle

Oracle Fusion 26B Introduces Agentic Apps With Ledger, Payables, and Expenses Agents

Oracle's Fusion Applications 26B release introduces Fusion Agentic Applications across the suite, with ERP gaining a Security Command Center and a roster of finance AI agents — including Ledger Agent, Payables Agent, Expenses Agent, and Payment Agent — alongside hundreds of product updates. It positions Oracle against SAP's Autonomous Finance and Workday's agent platform in the race to automate core finance workflows.

Why it mattersAll three major ERP vendors now ship named finance agents in the same quarter — the operating question for finance teams has shifted from whether to use agents to which controls govern them.

Jun 2026

Notable
CFO Connect

State of AI in Finance 2026: 56% of Finance Leaders Use AI — Still Last Among Functions

CFO Connect's State of AI in Finance 2026 report finds 56% of finance leaders now use AI — roughly double 2023 — yet finance still ranks last among business functions in AI deployment. Results to date are strongest in transactional finance (invoice-to-cash, procure-to-pay), with near-term investment attention shifting toward FP&A and financial reporting, and AI moving from standalone tools into ERP, FP&A, payroll, AP, and spend platforms.

Why it mattersFinance being the last function to deploy AI while doubling adoption in two years means the steepest part of the adoption curve is happening right now — and it's happening inside existing ERP/FP&A tools, not new apps.

Try itAudit which AI features already exist in your current ERP/FP&A/AP stack before adding vendors — the report shows embedded AI is where finance adoption is actually occurring.

JPMorgan

JPMorgan Pushes Tech Spend Toward $19.8B as Dimon Frames AI as Banking's Battleground

JPMorgan's projected 2026 technology expenditure is approaching $19.8 billion — up about 10% year over year, with roughly $1.2 billion more on major projects and platform buildouts — as Jamie Dimon positions AI as the competitive battleground for banking. The bank runs AI agents internally for coding and research across its 'fully AI-connected megabank' blueprint, though it has not yet opened its systems to external agents.

Why it mattersA ~$20B annual tech budget aimed at AI sets the spending bar for financial services — mid-size institutions can't match it, which pushes them toward vendor agents and embedded AI instead.

Quantifind

Quantifind Raises $200M for AI Risk Intelligence in Financial Crime Detection

Quantifind, whose AI risk-intelligence platform helps banks and agencies detect financial crime (AML screening, sanctions, fraud signals), closed $200 million in growth financing — part of a June wave of funding for AI applied to compliance, risk, and public-sector use cases. The raise underscores that regulated, high-stakes finance domains are attracting some of the largest applied-AI checks.

Why it mattersCompliance and financial-crime AI monetizes accuracy under regulation — a proving ground for AI systems that must be auditable, which is exactly the bar finance AI everywhere will be held to.

Bain: CFOs Funded the AI Revolution — Now They're Joining It

Bain & Company's 'CFOs Funded the AI Revolution. Now They're Joining It.' argues finance chiefs who bankrolled enterprise AI are finally applying it to their own function, with the strongest results in transactional finance and attention shifting to FP&A and reporting. It lands amid survey data showing 68% of CFOs raising IT and digital spending at the fastest rate in 21 quarters, much of it AI.

Why it mattersThe budget holder becoming the adopter changes procurement dynamics: finance-AI vendors now sell to a buyer who measures payback quarterly and will demand auditable ROI.

CFO.com: Most Midsized Companies Now Use AI for FP&A

CFO.com's coverage of Vena's research reports that a majority of midsized companies now apply AI somewhere in their FP&A process — a tipping point for the mid-market, where planning teams are smallest and automation leverage is highest. The catch remains integration: more than half of organizations report only moderate or limited connection between FP&A tools and their source systems.

Why it mattersMid-market crossing 50% AI-in-FP&A means the practice is now table stakes — the differentiator moves to data foundations and how well teams operationalize AI output into decisions.

Brazil's Pocket Raises US$11M to Take On Plaud in AI Voice Capture

Brazilian startup Pocket raised US$11 million to expand its AI voice-capture device and app — a homegrown challenger to Plaud — using on-device and cloud AI to record, transcribe, and summarize conversations and meetings. The round funds production capacity and a move toward physical retail as AI hardware for personal knowledge capture gains traction.

Anthropic Files Confidential S-1 with SEC, Signalling IPO Path

Anthropic submitted a confidential draft S-1 registration statement to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, the standard first step toward a public offering. The company has not set a timeline or target price, and the filing is subject to SEC review. Analysts estimate a potential IPO valuation in the $80–100 billion range based on recent secondary-market transactions and the Series H price.

Major
Nvidia

NVIDIA Open-Sources Cosmos 3 Omni-Model for Physical AI and Robotics

NVIDIA released Cosmos 3, a multimodal foundation model combining vision, language, and physics simulation for robotics and autonomous systems. Released under the NVIDIA Open Model License, Cosmos 3 can generate photorealistic synthetic training environments, predict object dynamics, and control robot arms with sub-millimetre precision in simulation. It ships with integrations for Isaac Sim, ROS 2, and OpenUSD.

Why it mattersA freely available physical-world foundation model dramatically lowers the barrier for robotics startups and research labs that previously needed to build or buy expensive simulation stacks.

1 Jun 2026

Notable
OpenAI

OpenAI Announces GPT-4.5 Retirement on June 27, 2026

OpenAI issued a deprecation notice for GPT-4.5, its experimental large-parameter non-reasoning model released in February 2025. The model will be removed from the API on June 27, 2026. OpenAI cited high inference cost relative to newer models and low adoption compared to GPT-4.1 and the o-series as the primary reasons. Existing callers are advised to migrate to GPT-4.1 or o3 depending on their latency and capability requirements.

Why it mattersGPT-4.5 had the largest parameter count of any OpenAI chat model; its retirement signals that raw scale without efficiency improvements is no longer a viable product strategy at OpenAI.

Major
NAISS🇸🇪

Sweden's Arrhenius AI Supercomputer Enters Full Production at NAISS

The Arrhenius AI supercomputer, operated by the National Academic Infrastructure for Supercomputing in Sweden (NAISS) at KTH Royal Institute of Technology, entered full production capacity with 4,096 NVIDIA H100 GPUs delivering 16 exaFLOPS of AI compute. The system is the largest academic AI supercomputer in the Nordic region and is allocated via the Swedish Research Council to universities and research institutions across Sweden.

Why it mattersGiving Swedish academics 16 exaFLOPS on a national system reduces dependency on commercial cloud for frontier AI research and positions Sweden as a top-five European AI research infrastructure nation.

Major
Microsoft🇸🇪

Microsoft Completes $3 B AI Datacenter Expansion in Sweden, Bringing Total to 200k+ Servers

Microsoft announced the completion of its $3 billion AI datacenter expansion programme in Sweden, adding two new halls at the Gävle campus running entirely on renewable hydropower. The expansion brings Microsoft's total Sweden server count to over 200,000, making it the hyperscaler's largest European AI datacenter footprint. The facility will serve Azure AI customers across EMEA with data residency in the EU.

Why it matters200K servers in a single country cements Sweden as Microsoft's primary AI processing hub for the EU market, with significant implications for Swedish AI policy leverage and local talent demand.

1 Jun 2026

Major
Câmara dos Deputados🇧🇷

Brazil's Chamber of Deputies Schedules Full Vote on AI Framework Law for June 2026

Brazil's Câmara dos Deputados scheduled a floor vote on the Marco Legal da Inteligência Artificial (PL 2338/2023) for June 2026 after the special committee approved an amended text in May. The current draft establishes a risk-tier classification system similar to the EU AI Act, mandatory human oversight for high-risk AI in public services, and a National AI Authority. Brazil would become the first major Latin American country with a comprehensive AI law.

Why it mattersIf passed, Brazil's AI law becomes the regulatory template for Latin America, affecting multinationals operating across the region and potentially triggering similar legislation in Argentina, Colombia, and Mexico.

Notable
Anthropic

Snowflake and Anthropic: Claude Runs Inside Cortex AI — No Data Egress, Full Governance

At Snowflake Summit 26 in San Francisco, Snowflake and Anthropic detailed the technical architecture of their $200M partnership: Claude models run inside Snowflake Cortex AI natively, processing customer data without egress, fully governed by Snowflake's row- and column-level security policies. Enterprises can invoke Claude via SQL or Python directly in Snowflake Notebooks. The integration addresses the #1 blocker to enterprise AI adoption in financial services, healthcare, and insurance.

Why it mattersClaude callable via SQL inside Snowflake means every data engineer can incorporate LLM-powered enrichment into ETL and analytics pipelines without any data leaving the governed environment.

Salesforce

Salesforce Signs Agreement to Acquire Contentful for ~$1.5B to Power Agentforce AI Content

Salesforce signed a definitive agreement to acquire Contentful, a composable content management platform trusted by over 4,800 enterprises. The deal gives Agentforce a native content layer capable of delivering AI-assembled, personalised digital experiences at scale. The transaction is expected to close in Salesforce's fiscal Q3 2027, pending regulatory approval.

Why it mattersAgentforce's ability to dynamically assemble personalised content at scale was its missing capability — Contentful fills this gap and makes Salesforce's AI stack more self-contained.

Notable
Alibaba

Alibaba Launches Qwen3.7-Plus — Multimodal GUI Agent Unifying Vision and Language

Alibaba released Qwen3.7-Plus, a multimodal agent model that unifies GUI and CLI operation across visual and text tasks in a single set of weights. On ScreenSpot Pro it scores 79.0% and Terminal-Bench 70.3%, leading the open-API GUI agent field. Available via Alibaba Cloud at $0.40/M input tokens with native vision for images and video up to 5 minutes.

Why it mattersA GUI agent that can see screens, click UI elements, and execute CLI commands in one model unlocks full computer-use automation — previously requiring multi-model pipelines.

1 Jun 2026

Notable
Microsoft

GitHub Copilot Switches to Token-Based Billing — Developers Report Bills Up to 20× Higher

GitHub migrated all Copilot plans to usage-based token billing on June 1, replacing per-request pricing with metered AI credit consumption. Each plan includes a monthly AI credit allotment ($10 for Pro, $39 for Pro+), with additional usage billed at model API rates. Code completions remain unlimited, but agentic features using frontier models triggered reports of bills jumping from $29/month to $750 or more, sparking developer backlash across Reddit, X, and GitHub forums.

Why it mattersToken-based billing brings AI coding tool costs into direct view for finance teams — likely to trigger more deliberate model selection and usage governance across engineering organisations.

GitHubCopilotPricingDeveloper ToolsMicrosoft
Read source

1 Jun 2026

Major
OpenAI

OpenAI GPT-5.5 and Codex Now Generally Available on Amazon Bedrock

OpenAI and AWS made GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, and Codex generally available on Amazon Bedrock on June 1, 2026, following an April limited preview. Pricing matches OpenAI first-party rates with no separate seat licences; all usage counts toward existing AWS cloud spending commitments. Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents powered by OpenAI also launched in limited preview.

Why it mattersEnterprise teams already on AWS can call frontier OpenAI models without separate accounts or billing, collapsing the integration barrier for production agentic deployments.

1 Jun 2026

Major
European Union🇸🇪

EU AI Office's GPAI Model Enforcement Powers Activate August 2, 2026

The EU AI Office confirmed that full enforcement powers for General-Purpose AI model obligations under the AI Act take effect on August 2, 2026. From that date the Commission can compel documentation, order model evaluations, and levy fines of up to €15 million or 3% of global revenue. All new models face immediate obligations; providers of models placed on the market before August 2025 have a grace period to August 2027.

Why it mattersEvery foundation-model provider serving EU customers must now demonstrate GPAI Code of Practice compliance or face binding enforcement actions starting in August.

EUAI ActGPAIRegulationAI OfficeEnforcement
Read source

1 Jun 2026

Landmark
French Government

France Secures €93 Billion at Choose France Summit — SoftBank Pledges €75B AI Data Centre Buildout

At the Choose France summit at Versailles on June 1, President Macron announced €93 billion in foreign investment pledges across 71 projects expected to create 15,600+ jobs. SoftBank committed up to €75 billion to build 5 GW of AI data-centre capacity in France by 2031 — the single largest AI infrastructure pledge in European history — with Phase 1 delivering 3.1 GW in the Hauts-de-France region. Additional AI commitments came from Brookfield ($10B data centre in Escaudain) and other infrastructure investors.

Why it matters€75 billion from a single investor into French AI compute is the largest AI infrastructure bet outside the US, positioning France as Europe's de facto AI compute hub and reducing bloc-wide dependence on US hyperscalers.

Try itTrack the Hauts-de-France data-centre buildout — these facilities will host NVIDIA hardware at European scale, creating a near-term opportunity for European AI labs to access affordable inference compute from 2027.

FranceAI InfrastructureData CentersSoftBankInvestmentEUPolicy
Read source

1 Jun 2026

Major
SPRIND

Germany's SPRIND Closes Applications for €125M Next Frontier AI Challenge to Build European Foundation-Model Labs

Germany's Federal Agency for Breakthrough Innovation (SPRIND) closed applications on June 1 for the Next Frontier AI challenge — a €125 million, non-dilutive DARPA-style competition to fund at least three European frontier AI labs. Up to 10 teams will receive up to €27 million each across three progressive stages; jury pitches are scheduled June 24–25, with Stage 1 funding beginning in July 2026. Winners are positioned for €1 billion follow-on rounds in 2028.

Why it mattersSPRIND's challenge is the EU's most direct attempt to build US/China-competitive frontier AI labs from scratch — the June jury process will reveal which European teams are credible contenders for sovereign AI development.

Try itMonitor SPRIND's jury pitch announcements (June 24–25) to identify the European AI research teams most likely to emerge as the continent's answer to frontier labs.

GermanySPRINDFrontier AIEUFundingResearchAI Policy

1 Jun 2026

Major
Mistral AI

Mistral AI Now Summit: Vibe Assistant, Industrial AI Platform, and €4B Compute Expansion Announced

At its inaugural AI Now Summit in Paris on May 28, Mistral announced the rebrand of Le Chat to Vibe — a broader organisational AI assistant targeting email, documents, and code workflows — alongside Mistral for Industrial Engineering, an integrated AI-plus-physics-simulation stack built partly on the acquisition of Emmi AI. The company disclosed 1,000 employees, a €1 billion 2026 revenue target, and a new 10 MW Les Ulis inference data centre (Q3 2026) powered by NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 accelerators. Mistral Compute, its infrastructure arm, targets 200 MW by 2027 and 1 GW by 2030 as part of a €4 billion programme in France and Sweden.

Why it mattersMistral's pivot from pure model provider to vertically integrated AI-plus-simulation stack for industrial engineering puts it in direct competition with Siemens and Ansys for high-value manufacturing workflows across European industry.

Try itEvaluate the Mistral for Industrial Engineering preview if your team runs finite-element simulations — the Emmi AI acquisition introduces physics-grounded generation that could reduce simulation iteration cycles.

Mistral AIFranceIndustrial AIData CenterVibeAI InfrastructureEU
Read source

1 Jun 2026

Major
Cisco

Cisco Study: Every Deployed Frontier AI Model Vulnerable to Multi-Turn Jailbreaks — Up to 88% Attack Success

Cisco's AI security research team tested 15 closed frontier models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Amazon, and xAI across ~30,000 single-turn and ~7,000 multi-turn attack scenarios. While single-turn attack success rates ranged from 2.19–64.91%, multi-turn ASR jumped to 7.89–88.30% — proving that conversational persistence systematically defeats all deployed safety guardrails. An open-weight companion study ('Death by a Thousand Prompts') found 92.78% ASR for Mistral Large-2. Cisco concluded standard AI safety benchmarks fundamentally miss the real-world multi-turn threat vector.

Why it mattersProving every currently deployed frontier model can be reliably jailbroken through conversation directly challenges vendor safety claims and regulatory compliance frameworks built entirely around single-turn evaluations.

CiscoAI SafetyJailbreakRed TeamingMulti-TurnSecurity Research
Read source

1 Jun 2026

Notable
Lovable🇸🇪

Lovable Launches Reusable 'Skills' Feature — Stockholm AI Startup Moves Beyond One-Off Prompting

Stockholm-based Lovable — which reached $400M ARR in February 2026 — launched 'Skills,' enabling users to save repeated instructions (design systems, SEO rules, accessibility checks) as reusable AI workflows. Five built-in skills ship at launch: /redesign, /accessibility, /seo-review, /video-creator, and /skill-creator. The update positions Lovable's platform as a specialized AI development teammate for enterprise teams rather than a one-off prompt tool.

Why it mattersSkills move AI-assisted app building from one-off prompting to systematic, repeatable workflows — a key step toward enterprise adoption of vibe-coding platforms.

LovableSwedenVibe CodingAI App BuilderStockholmDeveloper Tools
Read source

1 Jun 2026

Notable
AI Brasil🇧🇷

AI Brasil Day 2026 Brings 300+ Brazilian Business Leaders to Cubo Itaú in São Paulo

AI Brasil Day 2026 took place on June 1 at Cubo Itaú in São Paulo, convening approximately 300–350 C-suite executives from Apple Brazil, Google, Nvidia, LinkedIn, Bradesco, Magalu, KPMG, CI&T, Stone, iFood, and Semantix. Positioned as Brazil's practitioner answer to NVIDIA GTC, the event translated global AI breakthroughs into applied enterprise strategies covering agentic AI deployment, AI governance ahead of PL 2338's expected vote, and sector-specific use cases in financial services, retail, and agritech.

Why it mattersWith PL 2338 approaching a floor vote, this event set the enterprise compliance and adoption agenda for Brazil's largest companies — attendance patterns signal which sectors are accelerating and which are waiting for regulatory clarity.

BrazilAI EventEnterprise AISão PauloCubo ItaúBusiness Leaders
Read source

1 Jun 2026

Notable
European Commission

EU advances AI Gigafactories plan despite funding delays

The European High Performance Computing Joint Undertaking Governing Board agreed in principle on June 1, 2026 to proceed with the EU's AI Gigafactories initiative, with the Commission expected to launch an official call for proposals in July. The broader €20B plan for five AI data centers has faced delays and funding gaps that have alienated some potential industrial partners, according to Bloomberg reporting.

Why it mattersHighlights the gap between Europe's sovereign-AI ambitions and execution — useful context against the same-week CADA proposal and VivaTech infrastructure announcements.

EU PolicyInfrastructureFundingSovereignty
